Custom Poker Data & Vision Systems

Poker OCR to GTO Insights

Extract table data, reconstruct hand histories, and generate GTO suggestions, equity, and stats in one pipeline.

I build custom poker OCR and computer vision systems that convert table visuals into structured hand histories and analytical outputs. End-to-end pipeline: screen → hand history → solver → GTO suggestions, equity, and stats.

Demos
Fiverr gig Poker table OCR — screen to structured data fiverr.com/hasanaat/do-poker-table-ocr Open gig ↗

This site does not promote gambling. Systems are intended for analysis and research use. Offline modes are suitable for study and review. Clients are responsible for compliance with third-party platform terms and lawful use.

Overview

I build custom poker OCR and computer vision systems that convert table visuals into structured, usable data. The process starts with Poker Eye, which extracts cards, stacks, bets, and UI elements directly from the screen. Poker Reader then reconstructs complete hand histories by tracking actions, sequencing events, and handling missing or delayed information.

Those hand histories are mapped to solver logic using APIs, local solutions, or scraping methods to generate GTO-based suggestions. A dedicated stats and equity layer computes win probabilities and aggregates gameplay metrics, turning raw data into meaningful insights.

The system is modular and can run in real time or offline, depending on the use case. It is built for real environments—handling UI inconsistencies, animations, and the absence of direct data access.

Primary outcomes · Glossary

Primary outcomes

What engagements are optimized to deliver—pick the subset that matches your product.

  • Accurate extraction of cards, stacks, bets, and positions from screen
  • Clean, structured hand histories with correct action sequencing
  • Real-time or offline processing
  • Mapping of game states to GTO-based suggestions
  • Equity calculations per decision point
  • Automated stat generation from parsed hands
  • Reduced manual effort in tagging and reconstruction
  • Scalable data pipelines for analysis and training
  • Works without APIs; supports API integration if available
  • Handles animations, delays, and incomplete data

Glossary

Short definitions for terms used on this page.

Terms at a glance
OCR
Extracts text and numbers from screen images.
Computer vision
Detects visual elements like cards and UI components.
Hand history
Structured sequence of actions in a hand.
Poker Eye
Visual extraction module.
Poker Reader
Hand reconstruction engine.
Solver integration
Mapping states to strategy outputs.
GTO suggestions
Optimal decisions from solver logic.
Equity
Win probability at a decision point.
Stats
Aggregated gameplay metrics.

Process & timeline

From requirements through delivery—aligned to how modular pipelines are built.

  1. Requirement review + sample analysis
  2. Poker Eye development (data extraction)
  3. Poker Reader development (hand reconstruction)
  4. Solver integration (API / local / scraping)
  5. Stats & equity implementation
  6. Testing with real scenarios
  7. Iteration and final delivery

Modular pipeline & services

End-to-end flow is Eye → Reader → Solver → Stats/Equity. Engage for one module or the full chain—each block below solves a specific gap.

Poker Eye (OCR / computer vision)

Extracts cards, stacks, bets, positions, and UI states directly from table visuals.

Solves: Removes dependency on APIs by reading raw screen data.

Poker Reader (hand history engine)

Converts extracted data into structured hand histories by tracking actions, sequencing events, and resolving missing information.

Solves: Rebuilds full hand flow from imperfect visual input.

Solver integration + GTO engine

Maps hand states to solver outputs via API, local solutions, or parallel scraping to generate GTO suggestions.

Solves: Connects real gameplay data to optimal strategy decisions.

Stats & equity engine

Computes equity, frequencies, and aggregated stats from reconstructed hands and solver outputs.

Solves: Turns raw hand data into usable insights.

Offline analysis system

Processes videos, screenshots, or manual inputs to generate hand histories and analytical outputs.

Solves: Enables post-session review without live dependency.

Who this is for

Poker tool developers

Need Reliable data extraction layer.

Constraint No API or inconsistent formats.

Data / strategy researchers

Need Large, structured datasets with equity and decisions.

Constraint Manual collection is too slow.

Training software builders

Need GTO suggestions and stats.

Constraint Require accurate hand histories.

Analytics platform builders

Need Clean input data for dashboards.

Constraint Lack standardized data sources.

Demo videos & prior work

Loading demos…

Tech stack & architecture

  • C# (core development)
  • Custom OCR and computer vision pipelines
  • Modular flow: Eye → Reader → Solver → Stats/Equity
  • Real-time and offline modes
  • Solver integration via API, local data, or scraping
  • Optional AI model integration (GPT-style)
  • Windows desktop environments
  • Handles asynchronous UI and animations
  • Outputs: structured logs, databases, analytics-ready data

Designed for reliability, maintainability, and integration with your downstream stack.

Why custom vision work

Layouts, timings, and table logic differ by client and platform. Custom Eye and Reader logic target your environment—not a generic template—so accuracy and maintainability match what you actually run.

Engagements stay modular: Poker Eye only, add Reader, solver + GTO integration, stats and equity, or offline analysis—scoped to what you need.

Frequently asked questions

Do you need API access?

No. It works directly from the screen.

Can it handle animations and delays?

Yes. That is handled in the Reader logic.

Real-time or offline?

Both are supported.

Can it integrate with solvers?

Yes—API, local data, or scraping.

Does it provide equity and stats?

Yes, it can provide those.

Is it modular?

Yes—it is developed in modules.

What do you need to start?

Desktop video recording, expected hand history output formats, and preferred solution providers.

Start a project

Hourly preferred; milestone structure optional. Typical inputs: screenshots or videos, platform details, and expected outputs. Deliverables can be individual modules or a full pipeline.

If you need extraction, hand-history reconstruction, solver integration, stats and equity, or offline analysis, send a focused inquiry.

Ask a question

Short questions only—we will email you back when we can. Add your email if you want a reply.

Serious inquiries only. Please provide:

  • Platform and table screenshots or videos
  • Required outputs (hand history, GTO suggestions, equity, stats)
